Given a sequence of arguments, a dict from action name to argparse.ArgumentParser instance,
and the global parser as a argparse.ArgumentParser instance, give each requested action's
parser a shot at parsing all arguments. This allows common arguments like "--repository" to be
shared across multiple action parsers.

Return the result as a tuple of: (a dict mapping from action name to an argparse.Namespace of
parsed arguments, a tuple of argument tuples where each is the remaining arguments not claimed
by any action parser).

functions.name: borgmatic.commands.arguments.make_parsers
documentation:
Build a global arguments parser, individual action parsers, and a combined parser containing
both. Return them as a tuple. The global parser is useful for parsing just global arguments
while ignoring actions, and the combined parser is handy for displaying help that includes
everything: global flags, a list of actions, etc.

functions.name: borgmatic.commands.arguments.parse_arguments
documentation:
Given command-line arguments with which this script was invoked, parse the arguments and return
them as a dict mapping from action name (or "global") to an argparse.Namespace instance.

Raise ValueError if the arguments cannot be parsed.
Raise SystemExit with an error code of 0 if "--help" was requested.

functions.name: borgmatic.borg.compact.compact_segments
documentation:
Given dry-run flag, a local or remote repository path, a configuration dict, and the local Borg
version, compact the segments in a repository.

functions.name: borgmatic.borg.break_lock.break_lock
documentation:
Given a local or remote repository path, a configuration dict, the local Borg version, an
argparse.Namespace of global arguments, and optional local and remote Borg paths, break any
repository and cache locks leftover from Borg aborting.

functions.name: borgmatic.borg.umount.unmount_archive
documentation:
Given a mounted filesystem mount point, and an optional local Borg paths, umount the filesystem
from the mount point.

functions.name: borgmatic.borg.export_tar.export_tar_archive
documentation:
Given a dry-run flag, a local or remote repository path, an archive name, zero or more paths to
export from the archive, a destination path to export to, a configuration dict, the local Borg
version, optional local and remote Borg paths, an optional filter program, whether to include
per-file details, and an optional number of path components to strip, export the archive into
the given destination path as a tar-formatted file.

If the destination path is "-", then stream the output to stdout instead of to a file.

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.dispatch.get_submodule_names
documentation: Given a parent module, return the names of its direct submodules as a tuple of strings.

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.dispatch.call_hook
documentation:
Given a configuration dict and a prefix to use in log entries, call the requested function of
the Python module corresponding to the given hook name. Supply that call with the configuration
for this hook (if any), the log prefix, and any given args and kwargs. Return the return value
of that call or None if the module in question is not a hook.

Raise ValueError if the hook name is unknown.
Raise AttributeError if the function name is not found in the module.
Raise anything else that the called function raises.

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.dispatch.call_hooks
documentation:
Given a configuration dict and a prefix to use in log entries, call the requested function of
the Python module corresponding to each hook of the given hook type (either "data_source" or
"monitoring"). Supply each call with the configuration for that hook, the log prefix, and any
given args and kwargs.

Collect any return values into a dict from module name to return value. Note that the module
name is the name of the hook module itself, which might be different from the hook configuration
option (e.g. "postgresql" for the former vs. "postgresql_databases" for the latter).

If the hook name is not present in the hooks configuration, then don't call the function for it
and omit it from the return values.

Raise ValueError if the hook name is unknown.
Raise AttributeError if the function name is not found in the module.
Raise anything else that a called function raises. An error stops calls to subsequent functions.

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.dispatch.call_hooks_even_if_unconfigured
documentation:
Given a configuration dict and a prefix to use in log entries, call the requested function of
the Python module corresponding to each hook of the given hook type (either "data_source" or
"monitoring"). Supply each call with the configuration for that hook, the log prefix, and any
given args and kwargs. Collect any return values into a dict from hook name to return value.

Raise AttributeError if the function name is not found in the module.
Raise anything else that a called function raises. An error stops calls to subsequent functions.

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.monitoring.uptime_kuma.initialize_monitor
documentation: No initialization is necessary for this monitor.

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.monitoring.uptime_kuma.ping_monitor
documentation:
Make a get request to the configured Uptime Kuma push_url. Use the given configuration filename
in any log entries. If this is a dry run, then don't actually push anything.

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.monitoring.uptime_kuma.destroy_monitor
documentation: No destruction is necessary for this monitor.

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.monitoring.healthchecks.initialize_monitor
documentation:
Add a handler to the root logger that stores in memory the most recent logs emitted. That way,
we can send them all to Healthchecks upon a finish or failure state. But skip this if the
"send_logs" option is false.

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.monitoring.healthchecks.ping_monitor
documentation:
Ping the configured Healthchecks URL or UUID, modified with the monitor.State. Use the given
configuration filename in any log entries, and log to Healthchecks with the giving log level.
If this is a dry run, then don't actually ping anything.

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.monitoring.healthchecks.destroy_monitor
documentation:
Remove the monitor handler that was added to the root logger. This prevents the handler from
getting reused by other instances of this monitor.

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.monitoring.pagerduty.initialize_monitor
documentation: No initialization is necessary for this monitor.

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.monitoring.pagerduty.ping_monitor
documentation:
If this is an error state, create a PagerDuty event with the configured integration key. Use
the given configuration filename in any log entries. If this is a dry run, then don't actually
create an event.

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.monitoring.pagerduty.destroy_monitor
documentation: No destruction is necessary for this monitor.

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.monitoring.pushover.initialize_monitor
documentation: No initialization is necessary for this monitor.

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.monitoring.pushover.ping_monitor
documentation:
Post a message to the configured Pushover application.
If this is a dry run, then don't actually update anything.

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.monitoring.pushover.destroy_monitor
documentation: No destruction is necessary for this monitor.

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.monitoring.cronitor.initialize_monitor
documentation: No initialization is necessary for this monitor.

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.monitoring.cronitor.ping_monitor
documentation:
Ping the configured Cronitor URL, modified with the monitor.State. Use the given configuration
filename in any log entries. If this is a dry run, then don't actually ping anything.

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.monitoring.cronitor.destroy_monitor
documentation: No destruction is necessary for this monitor.

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.monitoring.zabbix.initialize_monitor
documentation: No initialization is necessary for this monitor.

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.monitoring.zabbix.ping_monitor
documentation:
Update the configured Zabbix item using either the itemid, or a host and key.
If this is a dry run, then don't actually update anything.

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.monitoring.zabbix.destroy_monitor
documentation: No destruction is necessary for this monitor.

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.monitoring.cronhub.initialize_monitor
documentation: No initialization is necessary for this monitor.

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.monitoring.cronhub.ping_monitor
documentation:
Ping the configured Cronhub URL, modified with the monitor.State. Use the given configuration
filename in any log entries. If this is a dry run, then don't actually ping anything.

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.monitoring.cronhub.destroy_monitor
documentation: No destruction is necessary for this monitor.

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.monitoring.ntfy.initialize_monitor
documentation: No initialization is necessary for this monitor.

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.monitoring.ntfy.ping_monitor
documentation:
Ping the configured Ntfy topic. Use the given configuration filename in any log entries.
If this is a dry run, then don't actually ping anything.

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.monitoring.ntfy.destroy_monitor
documentation: No destruction is necessary for this monitor.

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.monitoring.logs.add_handler
documentation: Add the given handler to the global logger.

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.monitoring.logs.get_handler
documentation:
Given the identifier for an existing Forgetful_buffering_handler instance, return the handler.

Raise ValueError if the handler isn't found.

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.monitoring.logs.format_buffered_logs_for_payload
documentation:
Get the handler previously added to the root logger, and slurp buffered logs out of it to
send to Healthchecks.

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.monitoring.logs.remove_handler
documentation: Given the identifier for an existing Forgetful_buffering_handler instance, remove it.

classes.name: borgmatic.hooks.monitoring.logs.Forgetful_buffering_handler
documentation:
A buffering log handler that stores log messages in memory, and throws away messages (oldest
first) once a particular capacity in bytes is reached. But if the given byte capacity is zero,
don't throw away any messages.

The given identifier is used to distinguish the instance of this handler used for one monitoring
hook from those instances used for other monitoring hooks.

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.monitoring.apprise.initialize_monitor
documentation:
Add a handler to the root logger that stores in memory the most recent logs emitted. That way,
we can send them all to an Apprise notification service upon a finish or failure state. But skip
this if the "send_logs" option is false.

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.monitoring.apprise.ping_monitor
documentation:
Ping the configured Apprise service URLs. Use the given configuration filename in any log
entries. If this is a dry run, then don't actually ping anything.

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.monitoring.apprise.destroy_monitor
documentation:
Remove the monitor handler that was added to the root logger. This prevents the handler from
getting reused by other instances of this monitor.

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.monitoring.loki.initialize_monitor
documentation: Add a handler to the root logger to regularly send the logs to loki.

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.monitoring.loki.ping_monitor
documentation: Add an entry to the loki logger with the current state.

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.monitoring.loki.destroy_monitor
documentation: Remove the monitor handler that was added to the root logger.

classes.name: borgmatic.hooks.monitoring.loki.Loki_log_buffer

methods.name: borgmatic.hooks.monitoring.loki.Loki_log_buffer.add_value
documentation: Add a log entry to the stream.

methods.name: borgmatic.hooks.monitoring.loki.Loki_log_buffer.add_label
documentation: Add a label to the logging stream.

methods.name: borgmatic.hooks.monitoring.loki.Loki_log_buffer.__len__
documentation: Gets the number of lines currently in the buffer.

A log buffer that allows to output the logs as loki requests in json. Allows
adding labels to the log stream and takes care of communication with loki.

classes.name: borgmatic.hooks.monitoring.loki.Loki_log_handler

methods.name: borgmatic.hooks.monitoring.loki.Loki_log_handler.emit
documentation: Add a log record from the logging module to the stream.

methods.name: borgmatic.hooks.monitoring.loki.Loki_log_handler.add_label
documentation: Add a label to the logging stream.

methods.name: borgmatic.hooks.monitoring.loki.Loki_log_handler.raw
documentation: Add an arbitrary string as a log entry to the stream.

methods.name: borgmatic.hooks.monitoring.loki.Loki_log_handler.flush
documentation: Send the logs to loki and empty the buffer.
A log handler that sends logs to loki.

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.data_source.mysql.make_dump_path
documentation: Given a base directory, make the corresponding dump path.

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.data_source.mysql.database_names_to_dump
documentation:
Given a requested database config, return the corresponding sequence of database names to dump.
In the case of "all", query for the names of databases on the configured host and return them,
excluding any system databases that will cause problems during restore.

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.data_source.mysql.execute_dump_command
documentation:
Kick off a dump for the given MySQL/MariaDB database (provided as a configuration dict) to a
named pipe constructed from the given dump path and database name. Use the given log prefix in
any log entries.

Return a subprocess.Popen instance for the dump process ready to spew to a named pipe. But if
this is a dry run, then don't actually dump anything and return None.

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.data_source.mysql.use_streaming
documentation:
Given a sequence of MySQL database configuration dicts, a configuration dict (ignored), and a
log prefix (ignored), return whether streaming will be using during dumps.

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.data_source.mysql.dump_data_sources
documentation:
Dump the given MySQL/MariaDB databases to a named pipe. The databases are supplied as a sequence
of dicts, one dict describing each database as per the configuration schema. Use the given
borgmatic runtime directory to construct the destination path and the given log prefix in any
log entries.

Return a sequence of subprocess.Popen instances for the dump processes ready to spew to a named
pipe. But if this is a dry run, then don't actually dump anything and return an empty sequence.
Also append the given source directories with the parent directory of the database dumps.

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.data_source.mysql.remove_data_source_dumps
documentation:
Remove all database dump files for this hook regardless of the given databases. Use the
borgmatic runtime directory to construct the destination path and the log prefix in any log
entries. If this is a dry run, then don't actually remove anything.

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.data_source.mysql.make_data_source_dump_patterns
documentation:
Given a sequence of configurations dicts, a configuration dict, a prefix to log with, the
borgmatic runtime directory, and a database name to match, return the corresponding glob
patterns to match the database dump in an archive.

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.data_source.mysql.restore_data_source_dump
documentation:
Restore a database from the given extract stream. The database is supplied as a data source
configuration dict, but the given hook configuration is ignored. The given log prefix is used
for any log entries. If this is a dry run, then don't actually restore anything. Trigger the
given active extract process (an instance of subprocess.Popen) to produce output to consume.

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.data_source.btrfs.use_streaming
documentation: Return whether dump streaming is used for this hook. (Spoiler: It isn't.)

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.data_source.btrfs.get_filesystem_mount_points
documentation: Given a findmnt command to run, get all top-level Btrfs filesystem mount points.

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.data_source.btrfs.get_subvolumes_for_filesystem
documentation:
Given a Btrfs command to run and a Btrfs filesystem mount point, get the sorted subvolumes for
that filesystem. Include the filesystem itself.

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.data_source.btrfs.get_subvolumes
documentation:
Given a Btrfs command to run and a sequence of configured source directories, find the
intersection between the current Btrfs filesystem and subvolume mount points and the configured
borgmatic source directories. The idea is that these are the requested subvolumes to snapshot.

If the source directories is None, then return all subvolumes, sorted by path.

Return the result as a sequence of matching subvolume mount points.

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.data_source.btrfs.make_snapshot_path
documentation: Given the path to a subvolume, make a corresponding snapshot path for it.

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.data_source.btrfs.make_snapshot_exclude_path
documentation:
Given the path to a subvolume, make a corresponding exclude path for its embedded snapshot path.
This is to work around a quirk of Btrfs: If you make a snapshot path as a child directory of a
subvolume, then the snapshot's own initial directory component shows up as an empty directory
within the snapshot itself. For instance, if you have a Btrfs subvolume at /mnt and make a
snapshot of it at:

    /mnt/.borgmatic-snapshot-1234/mnt

... then the snapshot itself will have an empty directory at:

    /mnt/.borgmatic-snapshot-1234/mnt/.borgmatic-snapshot-1234

So to prevent that from ending up in the Borg archive, this function produces its path for
exclusion.

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.data_source.btrfs.make_borg_source_directory_path
documentation:
Given the path to a subvolume and a source directory inside it, make a corresponding path for
the source directory within a snapshot path intended for giving to Borg.

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.data_source.btrfs.snapshot_subvolume
documentation:
Given a Btrfs command to run, the path to a subvolume, and the path for a snapshot, create a new
Btrfs snapshot of the subvolume.

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.data_source.btrfs.dump_data_sources
documentation:
Given a Btrfs configuration dict, a configuration dict, a log prefix, the borgmatic
configuration file paths, the borgmatic runtime directory, the configured source directories,
and whether this is a dry run, auto-detect and snapshot any Btrfs subvolume mount points listed
in the given source directories. Also update those source directories, replacing subvolume mount
points with corresponding snapshot directories so they get stored in the Borg archive instead.
Use the log prefix in any log entries.

Return an empty sequence, since there are no ongoing dump processes from this hook.

If this is a dry run, then don't actually snapshot anything.

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.data_source.btrfs.delete_snapshot
documentation: Given a Btrfs command to run and the name of a snapshot path, delete it.

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.data_source.btrfs.remove_data_source_dumps
documentation:
Given a Btrfs configuration dict, a configuration dict, a log prefix, the borgmatic runtime
directory, and whether this is a dry run, delete any Btrfs snapshots created by borgmatic. Use
the log prefix in any log entries. If this is a dry run, then don't actually remove anything.

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.data_source.btrfs.make_data_source_dump_patterns
documentation: Restores aren't implemented, because stored files can be extracted directly with "extract".

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.data_source.btrfs.restore_data_source_dump
documentation: Restores aren't implemented, because stored files can be extracted directly with "extract".

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.data_source.mariadb.make_dump_path
documentation: Given a base directory, make the corresponding dump path.

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.data_source.mariadb.database_names_to_dump
documentation:
Given a requested database config, return the corresponding sequence of database names to dump.
In the case of "all", query for the names of databases on the configured host and return them,

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.data_source.mariadb.execute_dump_command

log entries.

Return a subprocess.Popen instance for the dump process ready to spew to a named pipe. But if
this is a dry run, then don't actually dump anything and return None.

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.data_source.mariadb.use_streaming
documentation:
Given a sequence of MariaDB database configuration dicts, a configuration dict (ignored), and a
log prefix (ignored), return whether streaming will be using during dumps.

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.data_source.mariadb.dump_data_sources
documentation:
Dump the given MariaDB databases to a named pipe. The databases are supplied as a sequence of
dicts, one dict describing each database as per the configuration schema. Use the given
borgmatic runtime directory to construct the destination path and the given log prefix in any

Return a sequence of subprocess.Popen instances for the dump processes ready to spew to a named
pipe. But if this is a dry run, then don't actually dump anything and return an empty sequence.
Also append the given source directories with the parent directory of the database dumps.

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.data_source.mariadb.remove_data_source_dumps
documentation:
Remove all database dump files for this hook regardless of the given databases. Use the
borgmatic_runtime_directory to construct the destination path and the log prefix in any log
entries. If this is a dry run, then don't actually remove anything.

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.data_source.mariadb.make_data_source_dump_patterns
documentation:
Given a sequence of configurations dicts, a configuration dict, a prefix to log with, the
borgmatic runtime directory, and a database name to match, return the corresponding glob
patterns to match the database dump in an archive.

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.data_source.mariadb.restore_data_source_dump

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.data_source.bootstrap.use_streaming
documentation: Return whether dump streaming is used for this hook. (Spoiler: It isn't.)

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.data_source.bootstrap.dump_data_sources
documentation:
Given a bootstrap configuration dict, a configuration dict, a log prefix, the borgmatic
configuration file paths, the borgmatic runtime directory, the configured source directories,
and whether this is a dry run, create a borgmatic manifest file to store the paths of the
configuration files used to create the archive. But skip this if the bootstrap
store_config_files option is False or if this is a dry run.

Return an empty sequence, since there are no ongoing dump processes from this hook.

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.data_source.bootstrap.remove_data_source_dumps
documentation:
Given a bootstrap configuration dict, a configuration dict, a log prefix, the borgmatic runtime
directory, and whether this is a dry run, then remove the manifest file created above. If this
is a dry run, then don't actually remove anything.

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.data_source.bootstrap.make_data_source_dump_patterns
documentation:
Restores are implemented via the separate, purpose-specific "bootstrap" action rather than the
generic "restore".

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.data_source.bootstrap.restore_data_source_dump
documentation:
Restores are implemented via the separate, purpose-specific "bootstrap" action rather than the
generic "restore".

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.data_source.dump.make_data_source_dump_path
documentation:
Given a borgmatic runtime directory and a data source hook name, construct a data source dump
path.

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.data_source.dump.make_data_source_dump_filename
documentation:
Based on the given dump directory path, data source name, and hostname, return a filename to use
for the data source dump. The hostname defaults to localhost.

Raise ValueError if the data source name is invalid.

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.data_source.dump.create_parent_directory_for_dump
documentation: Create a directory to contain the given dump path.

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.data_source.dump.create_named_pipe_for_dump
documentation: Create a named pipe at the given dump path.

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.data_source.dump.remove_data_source_dumps
documentation:
Remove all data source dumps in the given dump directory path (including the directory itself).
If this is a dry run, then don't actually remove anything.

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.data_source.dump.convert_glob_patterns_to_borg_pattern
documentation:
Convert a sequence of shell glob patterns like "/etc/*", "/tmp/*" to the corresponding Borg
regular expression archive pattern as a single string like "re:etc/.*|tmp/.*".

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.data_source.lvm.use_streaming

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.data_source.lvm.get_logical_volumes

If source directories is None, include all logical volume mounts points, not just those in

Return the result as a sequence of Logical_volume instances.

documentation:
Given an lvcreate command to run, a snapshot name, the path to the logical volume device to

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.data_source.lvm.mount_snapshot
documentation:
Given a mount command to run, the device path for an existing snapshot, and the path where the

first).

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.data_source.lvm.dump_data_sources
documentation:
Given an LVM configuration dict, a configuration dict, a log prefix, the borgmatic configuration

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.data_source.lvm.unmount_snapshot
documentation: Given a umount command to run and the mount path of a snapshot, unmount it.

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.data_source.lvm.remove_snapshot
documentation: Given an lvremove command to run and the device path of a snapshot, remove it it.

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.data_source.lvm.get_snapshots
documentation:
Given an lvs command to run, return all LVM snapshots as a sequence of Snapshot instances.

If a snapshot name is given, filter the results to that snapshot.

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.data_source.lvm.remove_data_source_dumps

Given an LVM configuration dict, a configuration dict, a log prefix, the borgmatic runtime

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.data_source.lvm.make_data_source_dump_patterns

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.data_source.sqlite.remove_data_source_dumps

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.data_source.sqlite.make_data_source_dump_patterns

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.data_source.sqlite.restore_data_source_dump
documentation:
Restore a database from the given extract stream. The database is supplied as a data source
configuration dict, but the given hook configuration is ignored. The given log prefix is used
for any log entries. If this is a dry run, then don't actually restore anything. Trigger the
given active extract process (an instance of subprocess.Popen) to produce output to consume.

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.data_source.snapshot.get_contained_directories
documentation:
Given a parent directory and a set of candidate directories potentially inside it, get the
subset of contained directories for which the parent directory is actually the parent, a
grandparent, the very same directory, etc. The idea is if, say, /var/log and /var/lib are
candidate contained directories, but there's a parent directory (logical volume, dataset,
subvolume, etc.) at /var, then /var is what we want to snapshot.

Also mutate the given set of candidate contained directories to remove any actually contained
directories from it. That way, this function can be called multiple times, successively
processing candidate directories until none are left—and avoiding assigning any candidate
directory to more than one parent directory.

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.data_source.postgresql.make_dump_path
documentation: Given a base directory, make the corresponding dump path.

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.data_source.postgresql.make_extra_environment
documentation:
Make the extra_environment dict from the given database configuration. If restore connection
params are given, this is for a restore operation.

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.data_source.postgresql.database_names_to_dump
documentation:
Given a requested database config, return the corresponding sequence of database names to dump.
In the case of "all" when a database format is given, query for the names of databases on the
configured host and return them. For "all" without a database format, just return a sequence
containing "all".

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.data_source.postgresql.use_streaming
documentation:
Given a sequence of PostgreSQL database configuration dicts, a configuration dict (ignored), and
a log prefix (ignored), return whether streaming will be using during dumps.

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.data_source.postgresql.dump_data_sources
documentation:
Dump the given PostgreSQL databases to a named pipe. The databases are supplied as a sequence of

Return a sequence of subprocess.Popen instances for the dump processes ready to spew to a named

Also append the given source directories with the parent directory of the database dumps.

Raise ValueError if the databases to dump cannot be determined.

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.data_source.postgresql.remove_data_source_dumps
documentation:
Remove all database dump files for this hook regardless of the given databases. Use the
borgmatic runtime directory to construct the destination path and the log prefix in any log
entries. If this is a dry run, then don't actually remove anything.

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.data_source.postgresql.make_data_source_dump_patterns
documentation:
Given a sequence of configurations dicts, a configuration dict, a prefix to log with, the
borgmatic runtime directory, and a database name to match, return the corresponding glob
patterns to match the database dump in an archive.

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.data_source.postgresql.restore_data_source_dump
documentation:
Restore a database from the given extract stream. The database is supplied as a data source
configuration dict, but the given hook configuration is ignored. The given borgmatic runtime
directory is used to construct the destination path (used for the directory format), and the
given log prefix is used for any log entries. If this is a dry run, then don't actually restore
anything. Trigger the given active extract process (an instance of subprocess.Popen) to produce
output to consume.

If the extract process is None, then restore the dump from the filesystem rather than from an
extract stream.

Use the given connection parameters to connect to the database. The connection parameters are
hostname, port, username, and password.

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.data_source.mongodb.build_dump_command

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.data_source.mongodb.remove_data_source_dumps
documentation:
Remove all database dump files for this hook regardless of the given databases. Use the
borgmatic_runtime_directory to construct the destination path and the log prefix in any log
entries. If this is a dry run, then don't actually remove anything.

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.data_source.mongodb.make_data_source_dump_patterns
documentation:
Given a sequence of configurations dicts, a configuration dict, a prefix to log with, the
borgmatic runtime directory, and a database name to match, return the corresponding glob

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.data_source.mongodb.restore_data_source_dump

Restore a database from the given extract stream. The database is supplied as a data source

used to construct the destination path, and the given log prefix is used for any log entries. If
this is a dry run, then don't actually restore anything. Trigger the given active extract

If the extract process is None, then restore the dump from the filesystem rather than from an

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.data_source.mongodb.build_restore_command
documentation: Return the mongorestore command from a single database configuration.

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.data_source.zfs.use_streaming
documentation: Return whether dump streaming is used for this hook. (Spoiler: It isn't.)

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.data_source.zfs.get_datasets_to_backup
documentation:
Given a ZFS command to run and a sequence of configured source directories, find the
intersection between the current ZFS dataset mount points and the configured borgmatic source
directories. The idea is that these are the requested datasets to snapshot. But also include any
datasets tagged with a borgmatic-specific user property, whether or not they appear in source
directories.

Return the result as a sequence of Dataset instances, sorted by mount point.

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.data_source.zfs.get_all_dataset_mount_points
documentation: Given a ZFS command to run, return all ZFS datasets as a sequence of sorted mount points.

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.data_source.zfs.snapshot_dataset
documentation:
Given a ZFS command to run and a snapshot name of the form "dataset@snapshot", create a new ZFS
snapshot.

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.data_source.zfs.mount_snapshot
documentation:
Given a mount command to run, an existing snapshot name of the form "dataset@snapshot", and the
path where the snapshot should be mounted, mount the snapshot (making any necessary directories
first).

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.data_source.zfs.dump_data_sources
documentation:
Given a ZFS configuration dict, a configuration dict, a log prefix, the borgmatic configuration

directories and any dataset with a borgmatic-specific user property. Also update those source
directories, replacing dataset mount points with corresponding snapshot directories so they get
stored in the Borg archive instead. Use the log prefix in any log entries.

Return an empty sequence, since there are no ongoing dump processes from this hook.

If this is a dry run, then don't actually snapshot anything.

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.data_source.zfs.unmount_snapshot
documentation: Given a umount command to run and the mount path of a snapshot, unmount it.

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.data_source.zfs.destroy_snapshot
documentation:
Given a ZFS command to run and the name of a snapshot in the form "dataset@snapshot", destroy
it.

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.data_source.zfs.get_all_snapshots
documentation:
Given a ZFS command to run, return all ZFS snapshots as a sequence of full snapshot names of the
form "dataset@snapshot".

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.data_source.zfs.remove_data_source_dumps

Given a ZFS configuration dict, a configuration dict, a log prefix, the borgmatic runtime

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.data_source.zfs.restore_data_source_dump
documentation: Restores aren't implemented, because stored files can be extracted directly with "extract".

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.command.interpolate_context
documentation:
Given a config filename, a hook description, a single hook command, and a dict of context
names/values, interpolate the values by "{name}" into the command and return the result.

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.command.make_environment
documentation:
Given the existing system environment as a map from environment variable name to value, return
(in the same form) any extra environment variables that should be used when running command
hooks.

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.command.execute_hook
documentation:
Given a list of hook commands to execute, a umask to execute with (or None), a config filename,
a hook description, and whether this is a dry run, run the given commands. Or, don't run them
if this is a dry run.

The context contains optional values interpolated by name into the hook commands.

Raise ValueError if the umask cannot be parsed.
Raise subprocesses.CalledProcessError if an error occurs in a hook.

functions.name: borgmatic.hooks.command.considered_soft_failure
documentation:
Given a configuration filename and an exception object, return whether the exception object
represents a subprocess.CalledProcessError with a return code of SOFT_FAIL_EXIT_CODE. If so,
that indicates that the error is a "soft failure", and should not result in an error.
